target.title: fix(cron): add defensive prune before reading run log to prevent OOM
target.body:
> [!NOTE]
> This PR is AI-assisted (authored with OpenClaw/MillieClaw agent tooling).

## Problem

`readCronRunLogEntriesPage` reads the entire JSONL run log file into memory via `fs.readFile` before parsing and paginating. While writes are pruned to `DEFAULT_CRON_RUN_LOG_MAX_BYTES` (2MB) via `pruneIfNeeded`, the pruning is async and can fail silently under disk pressure. If pruning fails, the file grows unbounded, and a subsequent read loads the entire file into memory — causing OOM.

Closes #57017

## Root Cause

The `pruneIfNeeded` function is called in the write path (`appendCronRunLog`) but not in the read path (`readCronRunLogEntriesPage` / `readCronRunLogEntriesPageAll`). If the async prune during append fails silently (e.g. disk pressure, permission error), the file grows beyond the expected max size. The next read loads it all into memory.

## Fix

Add a defensive `serializedPruneIfNeeded` call before `fs.readFile` in both `readCronRunLogEntriesPage` and `readCronRunLogEntriesPageAll`. The prune is serialized through the `writesByPath` queue to prevent race conditions with concurrent appends.

Additional improvements driven by review feedback:
- `.catch(() => undefined)` on both prune calls so reads remain resilient under disk pressure
- Optional `pruneOptions` threaded from runtime config through the full gateway call chain, replacing hardcoded defaults
- 9 new tests covering the fix, error resilience, config threading, concurrency safety, and queue cleanup

## Testing

- Oversized file prune on read (core fix)
- Prune failure resilience — read-only dir (Page + PageAll)
- Custom `pruneOptions` respected (Page + PageAll)
- Concurrent append + read-side prune — no data loss
- Failed prune does not poison write queue
- Write queue cleanup after prune
